name: Board Meeting
summary: Comprehensive board update covering CEO commentary, key metrics, financials, functional updates, and forward-looking topics. Designed to keep directors informed, aligned, and ready to advise. Use the full set for in-person quarterly meetings; trim sections for monthly checkpoints.

beats.name: Introduction
beats.description: Set the tone for the meeting and walk through the agenda in one slide. Signal which sections are for discussion versus information so directors know where their time is most valuable.

beats.name: CEO Update
beats.description: Deliver the CEO's view of the period: what changed, what mattered, where the company stands. Two or three sentences in the speaker's voice — board members read this slide first.

beats.name: Highlights
beats.description: Share major successes across the business. Customer wins, launches, hires, milestones. Specific and brief; this is the slide that sets the tone, not the slide that proves the case.

beats.name: Challenges
beats.description: Address the obstacles facing the business honestly. Boards earn their value when surfaced to real problems; under-disclosure here erodes trust faster than the problems themselves.

beats.name: Company Needs
beats.description: Identify where the business needs the board's help: introductions, decisions, advice. Specific asks turn the meeting from a report into a working session.

beats.name: Funnel Metrics
beats.description: Discuss the sales pipeline: leads, conversion, velocity, win rate. Use the metrics that match the company's current motion; resist the temptation to show the same chart you showed last quarter for continuity's sake.

beats.name: Engagement Metrics
beats.description: Analyze customer interaction: usage, retention, NPS, expansion. Show whether the product is being loved or only being bought.

beats.name: Financial Metrics
beats.description: Review fiscal health: revenue, gross margin, burn, runway, cash. One slide directors can scan in twenty seconds and trust the numbers on.

beats.name: Performance vs Plan
beats.description: Compare actuals to plan across the metrics that matter most. Variance is the slide directors will probe; show your work, name the drivers, propose adjustments.

beats.name: Org Chart
beats.description: Present the team structure and any key changes since the last meeting. Directors track org evolution as a leading indicator; help them see hires, departures, and reshapes clearly.

beats.name: Product Roadmap
beats.description: Highlight the product plan and what ships when. Pair each major bet with the customer or business outcome it serves.

beats.name: Quality
beats.description: Showcase the product quality bar: reliability, defect rates, support outcomes. Directors take quality as a proxy for engineering culture; let the data speak.

beats.name: Engineering Update
beats.description: Communicate technical progress, key bets, and platform investments. Translate engineering work into business consequences directors can evaluate.

beats.name: Marketing Update
beats.description: Update on demand-gen, brand, and category-shaping efforts. Connect spend to pipeline and pipeline to revenue; avoid 'campaigns we ran' lists.

beats.name: Business Development Update
beats.description: Speak to growth initiatives: partnerships, channels, alliances. Each entry should have a stage, an owner, and a credible upside.

beats.name: Operations Update
beats.description: Discuss day-to-day operations: people ops, finance, legal, IT. Brief but explicit — directors notice when ops is missing.

beats.name: Special Topic 1
beats.description: Discuss the period's first deep-dive topic. This is where the board does its highest-leverage work; pose the question, show the analysis, ask for their view.

beats.name: Special Topic 2
beats.description: Cover the second deep-dive topic, framed the same way. Two specials per meeting; more, and the meeting becomes a recital.

beats.name: Feedback
beats.description: Elicit the board's perspective with concrete prompts. The strength of board governance is in its candor — invite it explicitly.

beats.name: Formalities
beats.description: Conclude with the necessary governance items: votes, approvals, committee reports, document acknowledgments. Run them efficiently and document the outcomes.

beats.name: Closing Remarks
beats.description: Close with concluding observations and the agenda for the next meeting. End on a forward-looking note; directors should leave knowing what to watch.
